explanation: This command matches the Sigma rule by using attrib.exe with the '+s' flag on a file in a suspicious directory ('\Users\Public\').
This command matches the Sigma rule by using attrib.exe with the '+s' flag on a file with a suspicious extension ('.exe').
This command matches the Sigma rule by using attrib.exe with the '+s' and '+h' flags on a file in a suspicious directory ('\AppData\Local\').
This command matches the Sigma rule by using an equivalent long option '+system' instead of '+s' and targets a suspicious directory ('\ProgramData\') with a suspicious extension ('.bat').
This command matches the Sigma rule by renaming attrib.exe and using it to apply the '+s' flag on a file in a suspicious directory ('\Downloads\').
